tasks.title: Auto-commit rally init and laps-hook install
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 1 (tasks 1.1-1.3). Dependency #2 (tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage) is archived/merged and the workspace tracked-file set has been cleaned, so `summary.jsonl` is the only churning tracked data file.

Context: openspec/changes/git-hygiene/proposal.md, design.md (Decision 1), specs/git-hygiene/spec.md ("Setup auto-commit"). Sites: cmd/rally/main.go runInit (~L367) and the laps.InstallHooks call (~L203).

Outcome: After `rally init`, the staged setup files commit once as `rally: initialize workspace`. After laps-hook install, affected files commit as `rally: install laps hooks`. Both use `--no-verify` and commit ONLY when something is actually staged (re-running init on a clean repo is a no-op).

Files & scope: cmd/rally/main.go only. Reuse existing gitx helpers (GitOutput, GitRepoRoot, GitUserFallbackConfig); do not shell out raw. Do not redefine the gitignore or the tracked-file set.

Design / risk: MUST NOT sweep the user's pre-existing unstaged changes into the setup commit — stage only the specific paths setup created/modified and commit just those (`git commit -- <paths>` or a staged-set guard), never a blanket `git add -A`. Skip the commit when the staged set is empty. Tolerate non-git repos and git failures gracefully (warn, do not abort init). Write the stage+commit step as a small testable helper that returns whether it committed.

Acceptance: `go build ./...` passes; `rally init` in a fresh git repo creates exactly one setup commit and a second run creates none. Tests are the next lap. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Tests: setup auto-commit behavior
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 1 (tasks 1.4, 4.3, 4.4). Follows the previous lap's code in cmd/rally/main.go.

Outcome: Tests for setup auto-commit, following existing repo patterns (see internal/gitx/git_test.go for temp-git-repo style). Cover:
- init in a clean repo produces exactly one `rally: initialize workspace` commit (1.4).
- re-running init with nothing changed creates no commit (1.4).
- init in a dirty working tree (user has unrelated unstaged changes) does NOT commit those changes — assert they remain unstaged after init (4.3).
- laps-hook install produces a `rally: install laps hooks` commit; re-running hook install does not duplicate it (4.4).

Files & scope: test files only. Do not change production code; if a behavior is untestable, add a head lap describing the needed seam instead of forcing it.

Acceptance: `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/gitx/...` (and whatever package holds the new code) passes. Concurrent-`rally start` duplicate-commit coverage (part of 1.4) is best-effort; if it can't be made deterministic, say so in the wrapup rather than skipping silently. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Lap-boundary commit instruction in done/handoff hook scripts
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 2 (tasks 2.1-2.3). Spec: specs/git-hygiene/spec.md "Lap-boundary commit instruction".

Context: internal/laps/laps-done-hook.sh (13 lines) and internal/laps/laps-handoff-hook.sh (15 lines) emit wrapup guidance the agent reads after `laps done` / `laps handoff`.

Outcome: Each script's wrapup output additionally instructs the agent to commit its work:
- done: message form `<lap-description>: done`
- handoff: message form `<lap-description>: in progress (handoff)`
Keep it advisory and consistent with the existing `echo` lines. `<lap-description>` is a placeholder the agent fills with the current lap's description; phrase the instruction so that is clear.

Files & scope: the two hook scripts plus their fixture/assertion tests (internal/laps/hooks_test.go is the likely home — match its assertion style). Do not alter the audit-trail or `rally progress` lines. If these scripts are embedded (go:embed) and installed, confirm the embedded copy is what tests read.

Acceptance: tests assert each script's output contains the commit instruction with the correct message form (2.3); `go test ./internal/laps/...` passes. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Leftover-work commit guidance at run start
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 2 (tasks 2.4-2.5). Spec: specs/git-hygiene/spec.md "Leftover-work commit guidance"; design Decision 3.

Context: internal/relay/runner.go builds the agent prompt via agent.BuildPrompt(opts) (~L797); BuildPromptOptions lives in internal/agent (see TaskPrompt handling ~L791 and the conditional incompleteRetryGuidance pattern ~L790). gitx.IsWorkspaceDirty (internal/gitx/git.go:67) already excludes `.rally/` paths.

Outcome: At run start, when the working tree has uncommitted NON-rally changes (IsWorkspaceDirty == true), the initial prompt dynamically includes advisory guidance to review and commit those changes before starting assigned work — code changes MUST be committed (upfront or folded into the end-of-run commit); docs/config-only MAY be left. When the tree is clean (or only `.rally/` files dirty), the guidance is omitted entirely.

Design / risk: Follow the existing conditional-prompt-text pattern (cf. incompleteRetryGuidance). Thread the dirty-check result into BuildPromptOptions cleanly; keep the guidance string with the other agent_prompt snippets rather than concatenating in the wrong layer. Reuse IsWorkspaceDirty — do not write a second porcelain parser. Tolerate IsWorkspaceDirty errors (treat as clean / omit guidance, never crash the run).

Acceptance: `go build ./...` passes. Tests (2.5): dirty tree outside `.rally/` -> prompt contains the guidance; clean tree -> omitted; only `.rally/` files dirty -> omitted. Add to internal/relay/*_test.go following runner_test.go patterns. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Verify phases 1-2 (setup commit + lap-boundary/leftover guidance)
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene. Verify the work so far (Phases 1-2): setup auto-commit, lap-boundary hook commit instructions, leftover-work run-start guidance.

Use the `openspec-verify-change` skill against change `git-hygiene`, then focus the report on tasks 1.1-1.4, 2.1-2.5, 4.3, 4.4.

Branch awareness: current branch is `dev`. Do NOT assume `main` is the merge target — determine it from the user, PR metadata, repo docs, or recent history, then diff with `git diff <target>...HEAD`. Identify the first lap/try of THIS batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing baseline (flag, do not remove). Do not rewrite git history.

Review with depth, not just green tests: confirm the setup commit stages only setup paths (open the commit, check it did not sweep user changes); `--no-verify` and the staged-set guard are present; both hook scripts emit the exact message forms; the leftover-work guidance actually toggles on dirty/clean/.rally-only trees (trace the prompt-assembly path). Report findings first; create head laps for substantive gaps; fix only tiny safe one-liners.

Check off the tasks.md boxes for the tasks you verified as correctly and thoroughly done; leave the rest unchecked and say why.

tasks.title: Fold rally state into work commit; retire CommitRallyState
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 3 (tasks 3.1-3.4). Spec: specs/git-hygiene/spec.md "No standalone state commit"; design Decisions 4-5. Highest-risk lap — it touches the run-finalization git path.

Context: internal/relay/runner.go — autoCommit (L1438) already does `git add -A` then commits `rally: run N attempt M (harness)`; the CommitRallyState call is at L1128 in the finalization path. internal/gitx/git.go — CommitRallyState (L92) and rallyTrackedStatePaths (L162). (`summary.jsonl` is now the only churning tracked data file, so `git add -A` folds it into the work commit.)

Outcome:
1. Confirm autoCommit's `git add -A` stages the summary.jsonl append, so no standalone state commit is needed in the common path (3.1).
2. Remove the CommitRallyState call at runner.go:1128 (3.2).
3. Add the amend-fallback for no-code runs, inline at that call site (3.3): if nothing is staged/unstaged -> do nothing; else if HEAD's commit message has the `rally:` prefix -> amend HEAD, appending ` [+state]` to its message; else -> create a single `rally: update state` commit (no stacking). Read HEAD's message via `git log -1 --format=%s`.
4. Remove CommitRallyState and rallyTrackedStatePaths from gitx/git.go entirely (3.4); keep pathIsGitIgnored only if still used elsewhere (grep first).

Design / risk: Decide amend-vs-new by the `rally:` MESSAGE prefix, NOT author identity (GitUserFallbackConfig is only a fallback; with a configured user.name the rally commit uses the user's identity and would not match). The amend must preserve authorship and use `--no-verify`. Never amend a non-rally HEAD. Audit for other CommitRallyState callers before deleting (grep repo + tests).

Acceptance: `go build ./...` passes; no remaining references to CommitRallyState/rallyTrackedStatePaths (grep clean). Tests are the next lap. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Tests: state-fold and amend-fallback scenarios
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 3 (task 3.5). Separate test lap because the amend-fallback git-state behavior is subtle and deserves an independent, thorough pass.

Outcome: Tests over temp git repos asserting the finalization commit behavior:
- Code-producing run: ONE work commit (`rally: run N attempt M ...`) that includes the summary.jsonl append, and NO separate `rally: update state` commit.
- No-code run with rally-authored HEAD (message has `rally:` prefix): HEAD is amended and its message gains ` [+state]`; no new commit.
- No-code run with non-rally HEAD: a single `rally: update state` commit is created (and a second no-code finalization does not stack another).
- No changes at all at finalization: neither amend nor new commit; at most a debug log.

Files & scope: test files only (internal/relay and/or internal/gitx, matching existing finalization test patterns). Drive through the real finalization path where feasible; if you must test the amend-decision helper directly, keep that as a focused unit test too. If a scenario is hard to reach through the public path, add a head lap describing the needed seam rather than over-mocking.

Acceptance: `go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/gitx/...` passes. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Verify phase 3 (state fold / CommitRallyState retirement)
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene. Verify Phase 3 immediately — it is the single highest-risk slice (run-finalization git path).

Use the `openspec-verify-change` skill against change `git-hygiene`, then focus the report on tasks 3.1-3.5.

Branch awareness: current branch is `dev`; determine the real merge target (user / PR / repo docs / history) before `git diff <target>...HEAD`. Treat pre-batch branch work as baseline. Do not rewrite git history.

Review with depth: CommitRallyState and rallyTrackedStatePaths are fully removed with no dangling callers (grep); the amend-vs-new decision keys on the `rally:` message prefix (not author identity); amend uses `--no-verify`, preserves authorship, and appends ` [+state]` exactly once (no double-stacking across consecutive no-code runs); the no-changes path is a true no-op. Run the Phase 3 tests and a realistic smoke (a tiny scripted run if feasible); confirm a normal code run yields exactly one work commit containing summary.jsonl, `git status` is clean afterward, and no stray `rally: update state` commits appear in the common path.

Report findings first; create head laps for substantive gaps; fix only tiny one-liners. Check off the tasks.md boxes for the tasks you verified as correctly and thoroughly done.

tasks.title: Edge-case hardening tests (special chars, git unavailable)
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 4 (tasks 4.5-4.6).

Outcome: Tests for the robustness edges of the new commit paths:
- Lap descriptions / commit messages containing special characters (quotes, newlines) are handled safely — no shell injection, no broken commit; the message is the literal content. Cover both the hook-script instruction form and any rally-side commit that embeds a description.
- git unavailable or repo-corrupted scenarios (not a git repo, or a failing git command) cause the new commit logic to log a warning and skip gracefully rather than crashing the run/init.

Files & scope: test files only, matching existing patterns (internal/gitx, internal/relay, cmd/rally as appropriate). For the git-unavailable case, prefer a non-git temp dir or a forced-failure seam over un-PATHing git globally.

Acceptance: `go test ./...` passes for the touched packages. If special-char handling reveals an actual production bug (e.g. a message built via unsafe string interpolation), add a head lap to fix it rather than weakening the test. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Docs and version bump for git-hygiene
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 4 (tasks 4.1, 4.7).

Outcome:
1. Document the new commit conventions in AGENTS.md and/or README.md (4.1): setup auto-commit (`rally: initialize workspace` / `rally: install laps hooks`), lap-boundary agent commits (`<lap-description>: done` / `: in progress (handoff)`), folded state (state rides the work commit; amend-fallback appends ` [+state]`; a single `rally: update state` only on a non-rally HEAD), and leftover-work run-start guidance. Keep it concise and consistent with the existing docs voice; place it where git/commit behavior is already discussed. Cross-check the wording against the implemented code, not just the proposal.
2. Bump internal/buildinfo/VERSION by one patch level (0.8.3 -> 0.8.4) per the release process in AGENTS.md (4.7). VERSION file only — do NOT create or push a git tag; auto-tag CI handles tagging on main.

Files & scope: AGENTS.md / README.md / internal/buildinfo/VERSION. No production logic changes.

Acceptance: docs accurately describe shipped behavior; `cat internal/buildinfo/VERSION` shows 0.8.4. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Final full-change verification of git-hygiene
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene — final whole-change verification before archive.

Use the `openspec-verify-change` skill against change `git-hygiene` and run the COMPLETE verification; inspect the whole diff.

Branch awareness: current branch is `dev`. Determine the intended merge target (user / PR metadata / repo docs / recent history) — do not assume `main` — then review with `git diff <target>...HEAD`. Identify the first lap/try of this batch; earlier branch work is pre-existing baseline (flag, do not remove). Do not rewrite git history; for any scope cleanup use additive/revert commits or ask the user.

Verify all tasks are complete and coherent across phases. Run `go test ./...` and `go build ./...`.

Review with depth: confirm clean `git status` during/after a run, one commit per lap boundary where the agent complied, no standalone `rally: update state` in the common path, and accurate docs. For task 4.2 (tracked-file-set), verify against the ACTUAL tracked set in the tree rather than any stale list — note for the planner/user: tasks.md 4.2 names `.rally/instructions.md`, which does not exist in this repo, and the `agent_status.jsonl` churn was a pre-batch old-rally-version artifact cleaned up before this batch; flag any remaining mismatch rather than editing the OpenSpec docs yourself.

Report findings first; create head laps for substantive gaps; fix only tiny safe one-liners. Check off the tasks.md boxes for every task you verified as correctly and thoroughly done.
